---
title: "Quest 3: Conditionals - Choose Your Path"
subtitle: "Make Decisions with if and else"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🛤️ QUEST 3 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/03-conditionals.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/03-conditionals.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Programs become useful when they make decisions.

## 💡 Rust `if` Basics

```rust
fn main() {
    let coins = 12;

    if coins >= 10 {
        println!("You can buy a potion.");
    } else {
        println!("Keep collecting coins.");
    }
}
```

## 👨‍💻 Example

```rust
fn main() {
    let temperature = 28;

    if temperature >= 30 {
        println!("Very warm day");
    } else if temperature >= 20 {
        println!("Comfortable day");
    } else {
        println!("Cool day");
    }
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Make a program that checks a `battery` value:
- 80 or more: "High"
- 30 to 79: "Medium"
- below 30: "Low"

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    let battery = 45;

    if battery >= 80 {
        println!("High");
    } else if battery >= 30 {
        println!("Medium");
    } else {
        println!("Low");
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 3 Complete
You used `if`, `else if`, and `else` to branch logic.
Next: [Quest 4: Loops](04-loops.qmd)
:::
